_readme: Content OS · M2 material source catalog — DATA ONLY. One object per source; add a source = add one object + redeploy, no code change. Full field reference, Bright Data mechanics and the collect-vs-discover / external_id traps are documented in readme.md — keep prose there, not here. NEVER put API keys in this file: 'auth_ref' names a Supabase secret (Bright Data uses BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY automatically). Knows nothing about pages — page wiring lives in page_material_sources / page_reference_sources / page_article_sources.

sources.name: tmdb_movie_list
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A. {list} = now_playing|upcoming|top_rated (same shape). Workflow A pulls zh-TW/TW + en-US/US = two page rows per list.

sources.name: tmdb_trending
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A. region optional — leave blank in the en-US page row.

sources.name: tmdb_search
sources.notes: Keyword search (RAZ-26 + RAZ-37). Matches TITLES only — no plot/keyword search.

sources.name: tmdb_watch_providers
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled, RAZ-24). Per-movie ({movie_id}) and returns results keyed by country, not an array — needs movie-driven routing, not a standalone list.

sources.name: article_rss
sources.notes: RAZ-25 · article feeds. url is EMPTY on purpose — the feed URL comes from page_article_sources.url and is assigned directly by buildArticleJobs, never templated: fillUrl runs every {placeholder} through encodeURIComponent, which is correct for a query param but would mangle a whole feed URL into https%3A%2F%2F... external_id is a FALLBACK CHAIN because RSS <guid> commonly carries an attribute (<guid isPermaLink="false">), which the XML parser yields as an OBJECT {@isPermaLink, #text} — mapping it as a plain path stringifies to '[object Object]' on EVERY item, collapsing the dedup key so only one article per feed per day survives. guid.#text first, bare guid for feeds whose guid has no attributes, link as last resort. topic_tags=category: RSS repeats <category> so it parses to an array. Only mode='rss' rows run here; mode='scrape' needs the async web-extractor path and is filtered out in loadArticleRows. Every feed host needs an api_rate_limits row or the gate denies it (no row = denied, fail-closed).

sources.name: article_scrape
sources.notes: RAZ-25 · article feeds, mode='scrape' (the no-feed case). url is EMPTY on purpose — the page URL comes from page_article_sources.url and is assigned by buildArticleJobs, never templated (fillUrl would encodeURIComponent a whole URL). Uses Bright Data WEB UNLOCKER (POST /request), NOT the datasets API: there is no per-site dataset for 'some news site', and /request is a direct fetch that returns in seconds — so unlike a discover job it fits the sync budget and needs no n8n. bd_zone must be a real zone (GET /zone/get_active_zones); 'mcp_unlocker' is BORROWED from the MCP's zone — a dedicated zone is a dashboard action if the two ever need separate budgets. Extraction is Open Graph first: every publisher emits og:*/article:published_time for their own share cards, so it is the closest thing to a universal article schema, and it is what the page ITSELF declares rather than our guess at which div holds the body; falls back to <title>/meta description. external_id=url (og:url canonical, falling back to the requested URL) — an article page has no id of its own. COST: the gate bills ONE REQUEST PER PAGE here (vs one per feed for RSS), so scrape is the expensive mode — prefer an RSS row whenever the site publishes a feed.

sources.name: reference_harvest
sources.notes: GENERIC ingest fallback. NOT used by the RAZ-36 harvest — that ingests under each platform's own bd_* entry so the platform-verified field_map (incl. composite engagement) applies; this entry's bare 'engagement' path would need n8n to pre-shape, i.e. per-platform knowledge in the orchestrator. tier=inspiration = reference ONLY, never verbatim (contract guardrail).

sources.name: bd_perplexity
sources.notes: AI-search. PROVEN LIVE 2026-07-16, ~78s — slowest of the five.

sources.name: bd_chatgpt
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~69s. bd_params.web_search=true — the scraper's own default is false.

sources.name: bd_gemini
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~30s. No web_search control.

sources.name: bd_google_ai
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~19s — FASTEST of the five, prefer as default. Optional 'hl' input sets answer language (add via bd_params).

sources.name: bd_copilot
sources.notes: AI-search. Proven live 2026-07-16, ~54s.

sources.name: bd_facebook
sources.notes: Social. FULLY CONFIRMED 2026-07-17 — BD's published dictionary (all 51 fields, owner-supplied) agrees with a 30-record payload re-audit on EVERY mapped key: url/post_id/content/date_posted/num_comments/num_shares/likes/post_type/attachments/hashtags. hashtags is 'Hashtags used in the post' (Array) and is string[] here (27/30, 28-distinct) — NOTE youtube's same-named field is object[], so the shape is per-platform, never assume. num_likes_type is an OBJECT (reaction-type breakdown, count_reactions_type holds all reactions) — NOT a count; the flat 'likes' is the count and is what engagement uses. Every page_* field (page_name/page_intro/page_category/page_logo/page_external_website/profile_id) is page-level and correctly unmapped. The ONLY collect-mode profile->posts scraper — hence no bd_discover_by (see readme). field_map CORRECTED against real harvests 2026-07-16 (10 posts, then 30 incl. 2 Reels): image_url=attachments.0.url — the ONLY image path that works for every post type (post_image is null on Reels; attachments.0.thumbnail_url is null on Photos; attachments.0.url is an scontent image for both, and the first frame for a carousel). post_external_image is the LINK-PREVIEW image, null on normal image posts. title=content (page_name is the page, identical on every post). A FB post has no title — title and summary are both the caption by design. kind=post_type so Reels/Videos are distinguishable from photo posts. media=attachments carries the whole array, so carousels keep every image and videos keep attachments[].video_url + thumbnail_url. play_count is deliberately NOT in engagement: engagement is SUMMED for best_performing ranking, and plays dwarf likes, so a Reel would win permanently (views != likes — same reason as the cross-source rule). page_logo/header_image/avatar_image_url are all page-level, never per-post.

sources.name: bd_instagram
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 posts) — it is this group's ONLY discover mode. field_map CORRECTED against real records: summary was 'caption' and image_url was 'display_url' — NEITHER key exists (both 0/10); the caption is 'description' and the image is 'thumbnail'. title was user_posted (the author) -> now description. kind=content_type. No shares metric on IG. TWO CAVEATS: (1) discover_by=url returns posts APPEARING ON the profile incl. collabs/paid partnerships — for interstellarmovie only 2/10 were actually authored by it (rest were paramountpics etc. with coauthor_producers=interstellarmovie). Instagram collabs legitimately appear on both grids, but 'reference page' means something looser here than on Facebook. (2) All 10 came back content_type=Reel; discovery_input exposes a post_type param we do not set — likely why no photo posts. Set it via bd_params if photo posts are wanted.

sources.name: bd_x
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 tweets). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was user_posted = the handle, identical on every tweet -> now description (the tweet text; X has no title). There is NO 'engagement' object — the counts are flat (likes/replies/reposts/quotes/views/bookmarks). views + bookmarks are deliberately EXCLUDED: engagement is summed for best_performing and views dwarf likes (same reason as Facebook play_count). image_url=photos.0 fills 2/10 — real, most tweets are text-only. topic_tags=hashtags: the KEY is CONFIRMED correct — BD's dictionary (owner-supplied 2026-07-17) documents 'hashtags' = 'Post Hashtags', Array. Population is UNVERIFIED, NOT broken: re-audited against all 10 stored posts and it is null on every one because this account posts no hashtags — do not 'fix' it by remapping. Element type is LIKELY string[] but unproven: BD says only 'Array', and the shape is per-platform (facebook 'Hashtags used in the post' = string[]; youtube = object[], flagged by its description naming BOTH the text and a search link). X's terse description matches facebook's pattern, not youtube's. If it turns out to be object[] it silently violates RawMaterial.topic_tags: string[] — settle it on the first harvest of a hashtag-using account. 'tagged_users' is likewise present-but-null here.

sources.name: bd_tiktok
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (10 posts, netflix). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was profile_username (the account, 1-distinct) -> description. No 'engagement' object; flat counts -> {likes:digg_count, comments:comment_count, shares:num_share_count}. play_count + collect_count EXCLUDED from engagement (views dwarf likes — same rule as FB play_count). kind=post_type. media=video_url. topic_tags=hashtags. carousel_images exists (null here) — TikTok photo-carousels would populate it; UNVERIFIED, this account posts video only.

sources.name: bd_youtube
sources.notes: Social. field_map VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against 10 real video records (@movierecapsofficial, order_by=Latest). CORRECTED against raw: image_url was 'thumbnail' — NO SUCH KEY (0/10); the real one is preview_image (10/10, 10-distinct). No 'engagement' object — flat -> {likes:likes, comments:num_comments}. views (10/10) EXCLUDED from engagement: it's summed for best_performing and views dwarf likes (same rule as FB/TikTok). title/description are real per-video (10/10, 10-distinct); youtuber, handle_name, subscribers and channel_url are all 1-distinct CHANNEL-level traps — never map title to them. kind=post_type is a constant 'post' on this dataset. topic_tags is left UNMAPPED deliberately: BD's dictionary defines hashtags as an Array of OBJECTS ('the hashtag text and its YouTube search link'), which would violate RawMaterial.topic_tags: string[] — needs a sub-path (e.g. hashtags.0.text) confirmed against a video that actually has hashtags; 'tags' was 0/10 here too. kind=post_type is the SHORTS DISCRIMINATOR per BD's dictionary — 'post' = standard video, 'short' = YouTube Short, 'N/A' — it reads 1-distinct 'post' here only because this channel is all long-form. media=video_url is a SIGNED googlevideo.com stream URL that EXPIRES — the durable link is 'url' (the /watch?v= page); treat media as throwaway. 'transcript' (10/10, full text) and 'category' are available but unmapped. REVERTED to the VIDEOS dataset gd_lk56epmy2i5g7lzu0k + discover_by=url 2026-07-17. The 2026-07-16 switch to gd_lk538t2k2p1k3oos71 was WRONG: that dataset is a channel-PROFILE scraper — a live capped run returned exactly 1 record (channel name/subscribers/videos_count, top_videos:[]) and 0 material written. It cannot reach a channel's videos at all; its only discovery type is 'keyword' (probe-confirmed). The videos dataset's discovery types are keyword|url|search_filters|hashtag|explore|podcast_url — 'url' is the channel->videos one, inputs {url, start_date, end_date, order_by, country, time_period, transcription_language}. order_by enum is CAPITALIZED: Latest|Popular|Oldest pass, latest/Newest rejected. Sort is NOT static: bd_sort_param/bd_sort_by_strategy derive it from the ref's strategy at plan time (latest_n->Latest, best_performing->Popular). The original unbounded crawl was a MISSING limit_per_input, not the wrong dataset — cap now supplies it. field_map still UNVERIFIED against this dataset's real schema — audit on first successful run.

sources.name: bd_youtube_search
sources.notes: YouTube KEYWORD SEARCH for the manual AI-assisted search tool (RAZ-37) — NOT the reference harvester (that's bd_youtube, same dataset, discover_by=url). Opt-in only: Bright Data is paid+slow, so buildSearchJobs excludes it unless named in body.sources. Uses discover_by=search_filters (probe-confirmed 2026-07-17), inputs {keyword_search REQUIRED, upload_date, type, duration, features, country, sort_by}. Chosen over discover_by=keyword because ONLY search_filters can express shorts-ONLY: its `type` enum is CAPITALIZED — Video|Shorts|Channel|Playlist|Movie all pass, 'short' rejected. Caller sets video_type — ALL THREE VERIFIED live 2026-07-17 (cap=10 each, checked against BD's OWN post_type output field, not the input filter): 'Video' -> 10/10 post_type='post' (no shorts); 'Shorts' -> 10/10 post_type='short' (no long-form); OMIT -> mixed 6 short + 4 post (genuinely both). Do NOT use video_length to tell them apart: shorts in that sample ran up to 164s. post_type ('post'|'short'|'N/A') is the only reliable discriminator and is mapped to kind. BD's own API-limitations panel warns 'Shorts do not ret...' and flags date-filter caveats (panel truncated in the dashboard screenshot — read it before adding upload_date/duration). Tradeoff: search_filters declares NO num_of_posts, so bd_search_cap -> limit_per_input is the ONLY record cap — never remove it. field_map is inherited from bd_youtube, VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against 10 real records of this exact dataset (same gd_ = same output schema): image_url=preview_image (NOT 'thumbnail', which doesn't exist) and engagement is flat {likes, num_comments} with views excluded. ASYNC-ONLY (mode=search_plan -> n8n Harvest Worker -> mode=ingest with sink=manual). MEASURED, NOT GUESSED: a live gated SYNC run (keyword='movie recap', video_type=Video, cap=10) took 112.6s, blew the 110s budget, and reported dataset_size:0 at cancel — ZERO records after ~2.5min. Not a near-miss: raising TIMEOUT_MS would not save it, because BD discovery tasks take minutes by design and must use POST /datasets/v3/trigger (their docs say so explicitly; sync /scrape auto-converts and times out). pullBrightData therefore REFUSES bd_input=search_filters, same as bd_input=url. Everything else was already proven on that run: payload validates, gate books it, key authenticates, field_map verified.

sources.name: bd_tiktok_search
sources.notes: RAZ-37 · tiktok KEYWORD search for the manual tool. Same dataset as bd_tiktok (the reference harvester) — only the discovery differs, so the output schema and field_map are identical and inherit bd_tiktok's 2026-07-16 verification. Async-only via search_plan (keyword discovery is a discover job). Input schema probe-confirmed 2026-07-17: {search_keyword REQUIRED, country} — NOTE the field is 'search_keyword', NOT 'keyword' like reddit/pinterest, and BD rejects unknown fields, hence bd_keyword_field. No result-type filter on tiktok, so video_type is ignored. bd_search_cap -> limit_per_input is the ONLY record cap.

sources.name: bd_reddit_search
sources.notes: RAZ-37 · reddit KEYWORD search for the manual tool. Same dataset as bd_reddit — only the discovery differs, so the field_map is identical and inherits bd_reddit's 2026-07-17 verification (10 real records). Async-only via search_plan. Input schema probe-confirmed 2026-07-17: {keyword REQUIRED, date REQUIRED, sort_by} — reddit is the ONLY platform with a SECOND required field: omit 'date' and the trigger is rejected, hence it lives in bd_params rather than being optional. Enums are CAPITALIZED (the recurring BD rule): date = All time|Past year|Past month|Past week|Today ('all time' rejected); sort_by = Hot|New|Top|Relevance. Defaults are 'All time' + 'Relevance' because a keyword SEARCH wants the best matches for the term, not the newest thing on reddit — unlike bd_reddit's subreddit harvest, where sort derives from the ref's strategy.

sources.name: bd_pinterest_search
sources.notes: RAZ-37 · pinterest KEYWORD search for the manual tool. Async-only via search_plan. Input schema probe-confirmed 2026-07-17: {keyword REQUIRED} — the simplest of the four, no other inputs at all. ** THE SAME DATASET RETURNS A DIFFERENT SHAPE PER DISCOVERY MODE ** — do NOT assume a search entry can inherit its harvester's field_map. bd_pinterest (discover_by=profile_url) verified 'title' 9/9, but keyword-discovery pins have NO 'title' KEY AT ALL, so the inherited map dropped all 7 records at normalize (title is required) and reported ok:true with material_pulled:0 — a silent total loss. Corrected against the real keyword payload 2026-07-17: title=[content, hashtags.0] — 'content' is the pin caption but only ~4/7 pins carry one, and hashtags.0 is a real per-pin value (e.g. 'Hollywood Poster Design') rather than a page-level constant. tiktok and reddit DID inherit cleanly (verified live), so this is per-platform, not a rule. BD also returns error records ({error, error_code:dead_page}, 3/10 here — pinterest keyword search is lossy); they carry no title and are correctly dropped.

sources.name: bd_linkedin
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=company_url. Discovery types are url|profile_url|company_url and ALL THREE take ONLY {url} — there is NO date, sort or num_of_posts input on LinkedIn (probe-confirmed 2026-07-17), so limit_per_input is the only cap and bd_sort_param does not apply. NOTE a /today/author/... URL is LinkedIn Pulse (that author's ARTICLES, post_type='article') — it is NOT a 'posts from today' filter; no such filter exists. field_map VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against 10 DISTINCT real posts (netflix; 13 stored rows = 10 posts fanned out per page — always de-dup on id before auditing, or every field looks 1-distinct). Mix was 6 image-posts + 4 video-posts, which is what finally settled two bugs the earlier N=1 sample hid: (1) image_url was video_thumbnail alone = 4/10 — it silently LOST all 6 image posts; now a fallback chain [images.0, video_thumbnail, document_cover_image] = 10/10. (2) title was post_text (the entire post body). Do NOT use the raw 'title' field despite its docs ('the title or main subject'): it is really the SEO page title, polluted with the company and comment count ('What We Watched | Netflix | 22 comments'). 'headline' is the clean per-post headline (10/10, 10-distinct) -> title=[headline, post_text], summary=post_text. topic_tags=hashtags VERIFIED string[] (2/10 populated — real, most netflix posts carry none) and NOTE the values KEEP the '#' prefix here, unlike facebook's bare strings. external_id='id' (post_id does NOT exist — dedup-breaking, fixed 2026-07-16). No 'engagement' object; flat -> {likes:num_likes, comments:num_comments} (LinkedIn has no share count). account_type is 'Organization' on all 10 — BD's dictionary claims 'Person'/'Company', so the doc is WRONG; trust the payload. kind=post_type is 1-distinct 'post' here (no articles from a company page).

sources.name: bd_reddit
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=subreddit_url; input schema (from BD validation echo) is url + sort_by + sort_by_time + keyword + start_date, url required. sort_by enum is CAPITALIZED — New/Hot/Rising/Top all pass validation, lowercase new/rising are rejected ('This value is not allowed'); probe-confirmed 2026-07-17. Sort is NOT static: bd_sort_param/bd_sort_by_strategy derive it from the ref's strategy at plan time (latest_n->New, best_performing->Top), because sorting New while ranking best_performing would rank posts that have no upvotes yet. field_map VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against 10 real records (r/movies, sort_by=New): title is a REAL per-post title (10/10, 10-distinct) — unlike every other platform, do NOT swap it; community_name is 1-distinct ('movies') and post_karma 1-distinct (0), both parent/author-level traps. summary=description is 8/10 (the 2 nulls are genuine link-posts with no body, not a bad map). No 'engagement' object — flat counts -> {likes:num_upvotes, comments:num_comments}; values are legitimately low because sort_by=New returns brand-new posts. NOTE: strategy=best_performing would need sort_by=Top/Hot — sort_by is per-source config here, so a best_performing reddit ref would rank near-zero counts. photos ('URLs of attached photos', Array) and videos (Array) are CONFIRMED against BD's published output schema (owner-supplied 2026-07-17) but were 0/10 on this link-post-heavy sample — the maps are sound, the sample just had no media; image_url=photos.0 relies on dot-index path resolution, UNVERIFIED end-to-end. topic_tags is deliberately UNMAPPED: the raw record carries a 'tag' key but it is absent from BD's published schema and 0/10 — Reddit has no hashtag concept. description_markdown (same text with Markdown/line breaks) and comments/related_posts (Arrays) are available if ever needed. community_* and post_karma/bio_description are all parent/author-level.

sources.name: bd_pinterest
sources.notes: Social. discover_by=profile_url PROVEN live 2026-07-16 (9 pins, imdb). field_map CORRECTED against real records: title was user_name (the account, 1-distinct) — Pinterest DOES have a real per-pin 'title'; summary=content, image=image_video_url (was 'description'/'image_url', neither exists). kind=post_type, topic_tags=hashtags. No shares on Pinterest; likes/comments_num are genuinely low/0 — real data, not a bad map.

sources.name: bd_quora
sources.notes: Social. NEEDS dataset_id — no screenshot yet. Errors defensively until pasted.

sources.name: bd_threads
sources.notes: DISABLED + needs dataset_id — confirm Bright Data offers a Threads scraper before enabling.
